ctx.name: Carlos
ctx.notes: For Carlos, this performance conversation needs to make the quality bar concrete and usable. I want to understand whether prior feedback translated into behavior changes or stayed abstract. I also need to hear how he defines good work right now and where he feels blocked in growth. Without that shared baseline, feedback can feel subjective and demotivating. My goal is to align on specific expectations, examples, and one near-term development focus he can practice immediately.

focusPoints.label: Concrete quality bar and what 'good' means
focusPoints.reason: What 'good' looks like in his day-to-day brand design work, so feedback isn’t abstract or subjective.

focusPoints.label: Whether prior feedback changed his behavior
focusPoints.reason: Manager wants to know if earlier feedback translated into observable behavior changes or stayed at the level of general advice.

focusPoints.label: How he defines good work right now
focusPoints.reason: What he thinks is expected of a junior brand designer today, so you can calibrate expectations together.

focusPoints.label: Growth blocks and the next practice focus
focusPoints.reason: Where he feels blocked in growth, and which near-term development focus he can practice immediately.

transcript.question: Before I share my view, how do you think the last stretch has gone?
transcript.answer: I can name wins, but I am not always sure they match the team bar.

transcript.question: When you say 'team bar' — what feels unclear about it?
transcript.answer: Feedback has helped, though I still miss consistency under time pressure.

transcript.question: Under time pressure, what specifically starts to slip in your work?
transcript.answer: I sometimes focus on polish and lose sight of the core message.

transcript.question: Can you walk me through one piece of recent work that you think met the bar?
transcript.answer: I want clearer examples of what excellent junior-level work looks like here.

transcript.question: What examples of excellent junior-level work would make the bar clearer for you?
transcript.answer: When reviews are broad, I am not sure what to change first.

transcript.question: When feedback is broad, what kind of direction is missing most?
transcript.answer: I would like to grow faster in concept rationale, not just execution speed.

transcript.question: Where do your standards for good work differ from mine right now?
transcript.answer: A tighter loop on one skill at a time would help me improve.

transcript.question: What part of concept rationale feels hardest to improve right now?
transcript.answer: If we align on concrete criteria, I can work against them confidently.

transcript.question: What is the first concrete change you want to make before we meet again?

I want clearer examples of what excellent junior-level work looks like here.
A tighter loop on one skill at a time would help me improve.
